title: [CI:DOCS] Refactor common options in man pages
body:
podman-create and -run have many options in common. To date,
these are copy-pasted and haphazardly maintained.

Solution: add an include mechanism, '@@option foo', such
that multiple md source files can fetch from one common file.

This is a Phase One commit, a very small subset of what's
possible. Purpose of this commit is ease of review. If this
passes review, much more (trickier stuff) will be forthcoming.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
Man page refactoring: add include mechanism for common options
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@containers/podman-maintainers this is a small subset of #14931. The purpose of this commit is that 14931 is impossible to review -- I had to make way too many manual edits to the existing inconsistent mess. With this commit, I want you to focus on the MECHANISM: the tool for including options. I've written it in Python to make it easy to add as a step to our [readthedocs process](https://readthedocs.org/projects/podman/builds/17501812/).

This subset of options was autogenerated by a pair of scripts I wrote for finding identical options; in this specific case, the descriptions, too, are identical. The whole goal right now is _primum non nocere_, confirming that this approach does no harm. To prove that, y'all can review the results via:
```console
$ git co main; make clean; make docs; mv docs/build/man{,.main}
$ git co pr/15174 (or whatever you call this branch); make clean; make docs; diff -ur docs/build/man{.main,} 
```
That proves that there is zero difference between the man pages on main and the man pages generated with this code.
```console
$ for i in docs/source/markdown/*.in;do f=${i%.in};git show main:$f | cdif -u - <(grep -v 'included file' $f);done
```
That proves that the only differences between the original `.md` files and the generated ones is whitespace. (See code comment for explanation).

Once (if) you're happy that this approach works, then please focus on the `Makefile` changes and the script itself.

If this merges, I will proceed with #14931 which adds a _lot_ more cleanup but will be trickier to review because our man pages have diverged from each other. That will then become a wording-and-phrasing PR instead of a complicated-new-mechanism PR.

Gulp - I hadn't expected this to merge so quickly. Now, who here groks `readthedocs`? It looks like [the build failed](https://readthedocs.org/api/v2/build/17618215.txt), probably because there's no "make docs" step (so `podman-create.md` and -run.md` don't get created). Who has the privs and know-how to add "make docs"?
I can take a look tomorrow, just make sure to not backport this to v4.2
Even easier than make docs: just run `hack/markdown-preprocess`. It will identify all `*.md.in` files and convert them to `.md`. (I forgot I added that).
I reverted the commit in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15186 as the PR does not pass the OSX Cross job.  Currently CI is red and reverting will buy us some time.
Rebasing our PRs as we saw: `Makefile:133: invalid 'override' directive`
@gbraad, let's wait before rebasing on https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15186. @Luap99 is looking into a proper fix.  I just took the shortcut :^)
I understand. It seems 'override' in the text is interpreted as a statement?
